mappings.rationaleAI: Confucian tradition addresses the Classical period of Chinese philosophy (B.2.e), Han state ideology (C.2.c.i), and T'ang cultural context (D.2.c).
Chinese literary culture addresses the character of Chinese civilization (A), Classical philosophy (B.2.e), Han cultural life (C.2.c.iii), and T'ang cultural developments (D.2.c).
Buddhism's spread into East Asia relates to the Han period introduction (C.2.c.iii) and Six Dynasties growth (D.1.b).
Central Eurasian trade relates to Han relations with the Hsiung-nu (C.2.c.ii) and T'ang foreign affairs (D.2.c.ii).
Chinese logographic systems relate to the character of Chinese civilization (A) and Shang script development (B.2.b).
Archaeological methods connect to archaeological sources (A) and the prehistoric period (B.1).
Dynastic succession connects to early dynasties (B.2), Ch'in and Han (C), and Sui and T'ang (D.2).
Provides broader context for understanding the foundational Chinese history covered in this section and its lasting influence.
